w20: Accelerator-optimized instance featuring four NVIDIA A100 GPUs and 340GB RAM for high-performance machine learning and AI inference tasks.
c150: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-4g: Accelerator-optimized server with 4 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 48 vCPUs, and 340GB RAM for demanding machine learning workloads.
c200: The Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized instance featuring 4 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 48 vCPUs, and 340GB RAM. It is engineered for high-performance machine learning and AI inference.
w150: The Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized instance featuring an x86_64 architecture powered by 48 vCPUs across 24 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 2.2 GHz. The system includes 340 GB of RAM and is equipped with 4 NVIDIA A100 Ampere GPUs providing a total of 160 GB VRAM. No local storage is bundled with this configuration. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, while LLM inference tasks demonstrate top-tier performance, particularly in prompt processing and text generation across various model sizes. The instance supports AVX and AVX512F instruction sets for specialized computation. With a starting on-demand price of 2.9579 USD per hour, this server provides a balanced hardware profile for compute-intensive tasks. It is specifically configured for machine learning model training, complex AI inference, and large-scale data science jobs requiring significant GPU acceleration.
